# Canal-Corridor Quarantine Dragoons

## What It Deepens

The Incident Wave (`TIMELINE.md` Layer 3) and the current-layer convergence (`TIMELINE.md` Layer 4) have stacked pressure on perimeter-enforcement capacity that the canon names but does not institutionally resolve. `INSTITUTIONS.md` §Law notes ward-breach response "often blurs religious and artifact-guild jurisdiction"; §Military names chartered watch and militia as the primary legal-force options; CF-0024 adds adventurer-contractors as auxiliary labor; CF-0025 adds occult-fallout specialists as post-breach containment. There is no dedicated rapid-response perimeter force in the canon today. The card proposes one — hybrid, militarized, civic-chartered, politically contested — and binds it tightly to the existing CF-0024 / CF-0028 regional-asymmetry pattern so it reads as another axis of the same Charter-Era stress-fracture rather than a new thing lifted from a different world. It also activates the dormant Charter-Era anti-monopoly-of-force tension that `INSTITUTIONS.md` names across Landholding/Military/Trade without until now having had a clean test-case: dragoons are the test-case.

## Why It Fits This World

Animalia is a world of Charter-Era strain. CF-0021, CF-0024, CF-0028 each established a regional-asymmetry pattern where polities respond differently to a novel pressure and the differential produces novel case law that the existing Charter framework cannot absorb. Quarantine dragoons are the natural fourth axis of that pattern — a polity-level adaptation to Incident Wave pressure that some polities adopt and others refuse, creating extradition and mobility-case disputes without breaking any invariant. Tonally, dragoons are not heroic: they evacuate families, seal quarters, chase brokers; they are feared, respected, and quietly avoided in the same register that extractors are (AES-1, AES-3). The unit fits the lived-in, hazardous, dryly political register of the world. It creates pressure rather than relieving it — more force around artifact work, not less, and contested legitimacy rather than settled authority.

## Immediate Consequences

In adopting polities (mostly larger heartland canal-choke-point cities; not all), ward-breach response time drops sharply. A quarter that would have waited hours for chartered-watch coordination with guild extractors now sees dragoons establishing cordon within minutes, with the extraction guild still arriving for the technical work. The speed is welcomed by residents and resented by the guilds, whose working rhythm has been rearranged. Chartered extraction guilds open negotiations with civic authority over the jurisdictional boundary — where does perimeter end and extraction begin? — and the answer differs per polity. Black-market artifact brokers who had used dragoon-active canal corridors re-route through non-adopting polities, sharpening the CF-0021 / CF-0024 / CF-0028 regional-asymmetry pattern into a fourth axis almost immediately. Bardic circuit picks up the subject matter within the first season: some bards sing ward-saved-the-quarter ballads under patron-commission (contracted-bard narrative-custody, CF-0024 extension), others sing Charter-violation laments. Principled-refuser clergy (CF-0026) in adopting polities mount visible critique; some progenitor-cult lineages denounce.

## Longer-Term Consequences

Over a generation, a new veteran subtype emerges — the retired dragoon — with career pattern distinct from mercenary veteran (CF-0024), occult-fallout specialist (CF-0025), and extractor veteran, and with charter-backed pension. The pension differential becomes a political issue: dragoons' charter-backed pensions contrast with the unstable welfare position of contractor-auxiliary widows and contaminated fallout-specialists, feeding reform-current clergy campaigns. Inter-polity extradition docket grows around cross-border dragoon pursuit — a unit from adopting Polity A chasing a broker into non-adopting Polity B raises the question of whether Polity B must honor Polity A's pursuit, and the answers vary. Magistrate courts across the heartland build case law painstakingly. In ruin-adjacent polities, a new pressure-vector emerges where CF-0028 ruin-levy mobilization, expedition-band skirmish, and dragoon perimeter response converge at the same ruin-mouth; magistrates build precedent with visible anxiety about which force has jurisdictional primacy. Mission-creep pressure is real and the Charter-Era anti-monopoly-of-force holds against it — attempts to expand dragoon brief into routine policing or external-conflict use are blocked by charter-court challenge, often brought by principled-refuser clergy. A small black-market in retired-dragoon kit appears; impersonation-of-dragoon becomes a named civic offense.

## Risks

The Charter-Era anti-monopoly-of-force strain is the card's intended pressure. It sits close to the AES-aligned frame (heroism paid in coin and scars, not glory; ordinary keeps the world honest) but does not cross it: dragoons are treated as paid laborers with a hazardous civic commission, not as heroic figures. The load-bearing stabilizer is the *perimeter / extraction* jurisdictional boundary — dragoons enforce perimeter only, chartered guilds retain extraction. If that boundary is not explicitly reaffirmed in canon-addition's CF emission, the card tips toward Charter-Era anti-monopoly-of-force breach and would require narrowing. Mission-creep into external conflict or routine policing would breach Charter norms decisively; the frontmatter disclaims this but canon-addition should require an explicit mission-scope commitment. Interaction with CF-0028 ruin-levy mobilization is a distinct failure mode — if the two forces are collapsed into one, the world loses a useful distinction; they must remain structurally separate in canon. SOC-4 is a stress point: brokers may push dragoons into contested enforcement territory. The *extraction-not-perimeter* boundary is the safety rail and must hold.

## Likely Burden If Accepted

Moderate to heavy. `INSTITUTIONS.md` §Military and §Law gain the dragoon unit language; §Trade/Guilds gains the perimeter/extraction boundary annotation; §Healing/Medicine gains a new veteran-injury pattern (dragoon-specific trauma). `TIMELINE.md` Layer 3 gains a dragoon-institution-founding note; Layer 4 gains the fourth-axis regional-asymmetry annotation. `GEOGRAPHY.md` §Regional Asymmetry on Expedition-Tolerance extends (or parallels) into a Regional Asymmetry on Dragoon-Adoption section. `ECONOMY_AND_RESOURCES.md` §Wage Spreads adds a dragoon tier; §Breakage Points gains dragoon-absence as an economic exposure in non-adopting polities. `EVERYDAY_LIFE.md` (a) heartland gains dragoon-presence texture; (b) big-city estate gains the political-standing-vis-a-vis-dragoons annotation. `CANON_LEDGER.md` receives the new CF record and a change-log entry; CF-0007, CF-0008, CF-0020, CF-0024, CF-0028 gain cross-reference annotations.

## Likely Story Yield

Very high — this is the highest story-yield card in the batch. Story engines activated: a dragoon commander facing a jurisdictional refusal from a chartered extraction-guild during an active breach; a black-market broker chased across a charter line into a non-adopting polity's protection; a retired dragoon returning to find his old unit deployed against a progenitor-cult demonstration; a principled-refuser clergy-member leading a charter-court challenge against dragoon mission creep; a ruin-mouth standoff where three forces (expedition-band, polity levy, dragoon perimeter) converge and the magistrate must adjudicate in real time; an impersonation-of-dragoon trial that reveals systematic kit-black-market corruption; a widow of a dragoon killed in ward-breach response navigating the charter-pension system while her neighbor's fallout-specialist husband gets nothing.

## Would This Be Better As

`soft_canon` as proposed. The contested-adoption asymmetry is load-bearing. Promotion to `hard_canon` would over-universalize and collapse the regional-variation pattern that CF-0021 / CF-0024 / CF-0028 have established as characteristic of the Charter Era. Demotion to `contested_canon` would under-institutionalize — in adopting polities, dragoons are not a disputed claim, they are a concrete body with uniforms and pensions.

## Canon Safety Check Trace

**Phase 7a (Invariants)**: All 16 invariants tested. ONT-1/2/3 respected (no sentience, magic, or interbreeding claim). CAU-1/2/3 respected — dragoons do not extract artifacts; CAU-3 (ward-inscription specifics restricted) is preserved because dragoons enforce perimeter, not inscription speech. DIS-1/2/3 untouched. SOC-1 respected (mixed-species recruitment; no species-coding). SOC-2 untouched. SOC-3 respected (pension-backed employment). SOC-4 respected only because the *perimeter / extraction* boundary is declared in-card — dragoons do NOT perform extraction, which remains chartered; this is the load-bearing stabilizer. AES-1 respected (dragoons are paid laborers, not glorified heroes). AES-2 respected (ordinary-life consequences — household evacuation, quarter-seal, bardic political debate — dominate the card). AES-3 respected (magical-contaminated alliance preserved — dragoons work in the contaminated register). No invariant breach. Anti-monopoly-of-force strain is declared and intended, within the canon's existing "strain but rarely break" framing.

**Phase 7b (Mystery Reserve Firewall)**: All 10 MR entries checked. M-1 / M-2 / M-3 / M-4 / M-5 / M-6 / M-7 / M-8 / M-9 / M-10: no overlap with the card's statement or consequences. Dragoons enforce perimeter; they do not investigate Maker-Age questions, make ontological claims, recover Maker fragments, or interact with guardian-constructions (if a guardian activates during an expedition, the dragoon response is perimeter-only; the M-8 mechanism question is untouched). No closures in any direction. Firewall intact.

**Phase 7c (Distribution Discipline)**: CFs consulted: CF-0007 (wards as public-but-restricted system — dragoons enforce without speaking inscription), CF-0008 (artifact trade guild-licensed — boundary preserved), CF-0011 (animal-folk any class — dragoon recruitment is mixed-species), CF-0015 (canal network — dragoons are positioned along the commercial spine), CF-0020 (local violent capability hireable — dragoons are chartered-civic, distinct from contractor hire), CF-0024 (contractor stratum — dragoons are institutionally distinct from contractors; card emphasizes), CF-0026 (principled-refuser tradition — likely political interlocutor), CF-0028 (ruin-expedition pressure — dragoon / ruin-levy distinction preserved). `recommended_scope: regional / current / public` with four concrete `why_not_universal` stabilizers tied to Charter-Era political economy. No contradiction with consulted CF stabilizers. No silent global implication — adoption is explicitly contested.

**Phase 7e Repairs Applied**: none fired.
